text.title: ADR Reversibility Tagging Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every adr-reversibility-tagging application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every ADR is tagged with EXACTLY one of the four reversibility values. No "tbd", no "depends", no custom tiers. The four tiers are deliberately discrete to force the tagger to pick the closest one.

rationale.source: Bezos 1997 shareholder letter (Type 1 vs Type 2); Annie Duke - Thinking in Bets (2018)

rule.rationale: Bezos's binary (Type 1 / Type 2) is the canonical version; the 4-tier expansion captures the partial cases that real-world systems produce. A continuous spectrum invites "it's a 5 out of 10" hedging that never produces a clear gate.

rule.statement: The reversibility tag is backed by a "rollback_estimate" field: estimated hours of engineering work + dollars + customer-visible impact. Tagger writes the estimate; reviewer sanity-checks. Tag without estimate is incomplete.

rationale.source: reference-class forecasting; Kahneman/Tversky planning fallacy work

rule.rationale: Untagged reversibility relies on gut feel which is systematically optimistic. Writing the estimate forces explicit reasoning. The estimate is auditable when the decision is reconsidered later — "we thought 1 week, took 6, lesson learned".

rule.statement: An ADR missing the reversibility frontmatter field is blocked from merge by CI. There is no "default" — explicit choice required. (Validate via validate-reversibility-frontmatter.py.)
rule.rationale: Defaults erode discipline. Blocking on missing field forces every author to consider the dimension. After 5-10 ADRs the team is fluent and the gate becomes invisible.

rule.statement: Approval depth scales with reversibility: two_way_door means solo decision, partial_two_way means one peer reviewer, one_way_door_costly means architect + product sign-off + 24h cool-off, one_way_door_irrevocable means founder sign-off + pre-mortem + 48h cool-off.

rationale.source: Bezos Type 1 vs Type 2; Amazon disagree-and-commit mechanics

rule.rationale: Matching gate depth to reversal cost prevents both over-process on two-way doors and under-process on one-way doors. The cool-off period prevents in-meeting commitment to irreversible moves.

rule.statement: Every ADR tagged one_way_door_costly or one_way_door_irrevocable includes a pre-mortem section answering: it is 12 months from now and this decision was wrong, what went wrong and what signal could have caught it earlier. Pre-mortem must name at least 2 reversal-trigger signals.

rationale.source: Gary Klein - pre-mortem technique (2007); Annie Duke

rule.rationale: Pre-mortems surface failure modes that the optimistic decision-frame hides. Naming reversal triggers creates accountability; if a signal fires later the team has a pre-committed protocol instead of denial.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
